findings.title: Feature-catalog pointer in code comment
findings.standard_violated: sk-code universal_patterns.md §4 forbids feature-catalog entries in code comments; comments must keep durable WHY only.
findings.fix_hint: Remove the catalog pointer or replace it with durable behavior rationale.

findings.title: Spec path embedded in code comment
findings.standard_violated: comment-hygiene.md lines 34-38 forbids spec-folder paths in code comments; keep durable WHY only.
findings.fix_hint: Describe the path shape generically without naming the spec path segment.

findings.title: Ephemeral fix id in code comment
findings.standard_violated: comment-hygiene.md and sk-code universal_patterns.md §4 forbid task/finding/ticket-style ids in code comments.
findings.fix_hint: Drop the fix id and keep only the durable intent-confidence rationale.

findings.title: Non-null assertion without justification
findings.standard_violated: TypeScript checklist P1: non-null assertions must have a preceding justification comment or be replaced with explicit narrowing.
findings.fix_hint: Introduce a narrowed local value or add a durable invariant comment immediately above.

findings.title: Non-null assertion without justification
findings.standard_violated: TypeScript checklist P1: non-null assertions must have a preceding justification comment or be replaced with explicit narrowing.
findings.fix_hint: Avoid detectedIntent! by guarding or defaulting before indexing INTENT_TO_MODE.

findings.title: Non-null assertion without justification
findings.standard_violated: TypeScript checklist P1: non-null assertions must have a preceding justification comment or be replaced with explicit narrowing.
findings.fix_hint: Narrow parsed.causalLinks inside the runner before calling processCausalLinks.

findings.title: Exported function lacks TSDoc
findings.standard_violated: TypeScript checklist P1: exported functions, classes, and interfaces have TSDoc comments.
findings.fix_hint: Add a short TSDoc purpose comment above the exported helper.

findings.title: Mixed type/value import
findings.standard_violated: TypeScript style guide §7 and checklist P2 require type-only imports to use import type and be separated from value imports.
findings.fix_hint: Split QueryPlan into a separate import type line in the type-only import group.

findings.title: Section numbering starts at zero
findings.standard_violated: OpenCode code_organization.md §2 requires the first numbered code section to start at 1 and increment sequentially.
findings.fix_hint: Renumber DEPENDENCIES to section 1 and update following section numbers.

findings.title: Section numbering skips a section
findings.standard_violated: OpenCode code_organization.md §2 requires numbered code sections to increment sequentially from sections present.
findings.fix_hint: Renumber the TYPES section or add the missing section divider.

findings.title: Unnumbered section divider
findings.standard_violated: TypeScript checklist requires numbered ALL-CAPS section headers; code_organization.md says significant files should use numbered sections.
findings.fix_hint: Change IMPORTS/CONSTANTS dividers to numbered section headers.

findings.title: Oversized handler module
findings.standard_violated: sk-code universal_patterns.md file-length guideline recommends splitting handler/endpoint files above 300 lines by domain.
findings.fix_hint: Continue extracting save subdomains until the legacy orchestration wrapper is small and behavior-focused.

findings.title: Feature-catalog comments violate comment hygiene
findings.standard_violated: comment-hygiene.md:36-38 and sk-code universal_checklist.md:79-85 require durable WHY comments only and forbid feature-catalog/artifact pointers in code comments.
findings.fix_hint: Remove the feature-catalog labels or replace them with a durable API-purpose comment.

findings.title: Feature-catalog comments violate comment hygiene

findings.title: Feature and fix identifiers in comments

findings.fix_hint: Remove feature/fix labels; keep the durable explanation about preserving non-embedded rows and content sync.

findings.title: Feature-catalog comments violate comment hygiene
findings.standard_violated: comment-hygiene.md:36-38 and sk-code universal_checklist.md:79-85 require durable WHY comments only and forbid feature-catalog/artifact pointers in code comments.

findings.title: Numbered sections are out of order

findings.title: Exported functions lack TSDoc

findings.title: Type-only imports are not grouped last
findings.standard_violated: sk-code code_organization.md:247-270 and typescript_checklist.md:305-320 require type-only imports to use import type and be grouped after runtime imports.

findings.title: Probable ephemeral fix label in comment
findings.standard_violated: Likely comment-hygiene.md:36-38 finding-id rule; if A1 is not a stable external standard, it should not appear in a code comment.

findings.title: Ephemeral fix identifier in code comment

findings.title: Ephemeral severity/id label in comment

findings.fix_hint: Use `catch (err: unknown)` for all catch blocks in this file.

findings.title: Import groups are not in sk-code TypeScript order
findings.standard_violated: TypeScript import order: Node built-ins, third-party packages, local modules, then type-only imports last.
findings.fix_hint: Group third-party imports before local imports and move type-only imports to the final import block.

findings.title: Schema module exceeds sk-code file length guidance

findings.title: Large governance module lacks numbered sections

findings.title: Feature-catalog comment pointer
findings.standard_violated: sk-code universal comment rule: feature-catalog entries are ephemeral artifact pointers; comments keep durable WHY only.

findings.title: Unnumbered section dividers

findings.title: Exported functions lack TSDoc

findings.evidence: .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/shared/paths.ts:71 and :91 export public functions without preceding TSDoc.

findings.title: Exported interfaces lack TSDoc

findings.fix_hint: Document the public ownership-map interfaces or mark them internal if not public API.

findings.title: Exported helper lacks public TSDoc

findings.fix_hint: Add TSDoc before buildWeightedDocumentText or remove it from the public export surface.

findings.title: Type-only imports not separated
findings.standard_violated: TypeScript checklist P2: imports used only in type positions use separated `import type`; quick reference puts type-only imports last.
findings.evidence: .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:9 imports a type before runtime imports; :66, :92, :122, and :126 mix type specifiers into runtime imports.
findings.fix_hint: Move type-only imports into a final import group.

findings.title: Unjustified any at MCP boundary
findings.standard_violated: TypeScript quality: prefer unknown over any on public surfaces; permitted any interop needs a justification comment.
findings.evidence: .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1049 disables no-explicit-any, :1050 returns Promise<any>, and :1364 casts as any without an SDK-interop rationale.
findings.fix_hint: Use a local MCP response type or add a precise SDK interop justification.

findings.title: compiled dist is stale against source security path
findings.standard_violated: TypeScript Quality Standards: compiled dist output must be rebuilt because CLI scripts run from dist; Universal OpenCode checklist: filesystem/spec-folder operations must enforce approved-root containment
findings.evidence: .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist/memory/generate-context.js:282 returns any existing absolute path, while .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/memory/generate-context.ts:355 validates absolute paths with validateFilePath
findings.fix_hint: Rebuild dist from current TypeScript source and verify generated output preserves the containment guard.

findings.title: ephemeral issue id in code comment
findings.standard_violated: Comment Hygiene hard block: comments must not embed ticket/finding/task/requirement identifiers; keep durable WHY only
findings.fix_hint: Replace the ISS marker with the durable rationale for the transaction.

findings.title: mjs script lacks OpenCode JavaScript box header
findings.standard_violated: JavaScript checklist: JavaScript files require a boxed component header; .mjs is exempt from use strict, not from the header
findings.fix_hint: Add the standard boxed COMPONENT/PURPOSE header after the shebang.

findings.title: legacy ASCII module header
findings.standard_violated: TypeScript Style Guide: TypeScript files must use the OpenCode module header block with box-drawing separator lines
findings.fix_hint: Normalize memory script headers to the required TypeScript MODULE header format.

findings.title: type-only import appears before value imports
findings.standard_violated: TypeScript Style Guide: type-only imports are separate and always last in the import block
findings.fix_hint: Move import type lines after all runtime imports.

findings.title: duplicate first numbered section
findings.standard_violated: OpenCode code organization: numbered section dividers start at 1 and increment sequentially
findings.fix_hint: Remove the redundant top section or renumber sections sequentially.

findings.title: Shell strict mode incomplete
findings.standard_violated: Shell checklist P0: strict mode must be `set -euo pipefail` near the top of every shell script.
findings.fix_hint: Use `set -euo pipefail`, not only `set -o pipefail`.

findings.title: Missing component header block

findings.title: Ephemeral decision pointer in code comment
findings.standard_violated: Comment Hygiene: never embed packet/phase numbers, ADR/task/checklist/requirement/finding ids, or other perishable artifact pointers in code comments; keep durable WHY only.
findings.fix_hint: Replace `008/D9` with the durable rationale for the thresholds.

findings.title: JavaScript file lacks required boxed header
findings.standard_violated: JavaScript checklist P0: JavaScript and .mjs files must have a boxed component header identifying the component.
findings.fix_hint: Add the required component/purpose boxed header after the shebang.

findings.title: Strict directive present in ESM file
findings.standard_violated: JavaScript style guide: do not add `'use strict'` to `.mjs`; ES modules are strict by definition.
findings.fix_hint: Remove `'use strict';` from the .mjs bridge.

findings.title: Catch variable lacks explicit unknown type
findings.standard_violated: TypeScript checklist P1: catch blocks use `unknown` type and narrow with `instanceof`.
findings.fix_hint: Use `catch (error: unknown)`.

findings.title: Exported handler lacks TSDoc
findings.standard_violated: TypeScript checklist P1: exported functions, classes, and interfaces have TSDoc comments.
findings.fix_hint: Add a short TSDoc purpose comment above `handleCodeGraphApply`.

findings.title: Possible ephemeral artifact id in comment
findings.standard_violated: Comment Hygiene: comments must not cite perishable ticket/feature/task identifiers; if `CG-003` is not a durable code enum, this violates the hard comment rule.
findings.fix_hint: Replace `CG-003` with the durable transaction-safety rationale or document why it is a stable code value.

findings.title: Type-only import is not last
findings.standard_violated: TypeScript style guide: imports follow Node built-ins, third-party, local modules, then type-only imports last with separation.
findings.fix_hint: Move the `import type` after runtime local imports.

findings.title: Exported public interfaces lack TSDoc
findings.standard_violated: sk-code TypeScript checklist P1: exported functions, classes, and interfaces have TSDoc comments.
findings.fix_hint: Add TSDoc to exported interfaces/types or keep non-public contracts unexported.

findings.title: Exported function lacks explicit return type
findings.standard_violated: sk-code TypeScript checklist P1: all exported functions have explicit return type annotations.
findings.fix_hint: Annotate the return type, e.g. the return type of buildReadinessBlock.

findings.title: Catch variables are not explicitly typed unknown
findings.standard_violated: sk-code TypeScript checklist P1: catch blocks use unknown type and narrow with instanceof.
findings.evidence: .opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/lib/query-result-adapter.ts:107; also observed in gold-query-verifier.ts:371,425, recovery-procedures.ts:231,281,333,401,426, apply-orchestrator.ts:597,707, code-graph-db.ts:521, tree-sitter-parser.ts:160, phase-runner.ts:297
findings.fix_hint: Use catch (error: unknown) or catch (cause: unknown) and keep the existing narrowing.

findings.title: Non-null assertions lack preceding justification
findings.standard_violated: sk-code TypeScript checklist P1: non-null assertions have a justification comment on the preceding line.
findings.fix_hint: Replace with explicit guards or add concise preceding WHY comments.

findings.title: Type-only imports are mixed into value imports
findings.standard_violated: sk-code TypeScript style: type-only imports use import type and are separated in the final import group.
findings.fix_hint: Move type-only imports to separate import type statements after value imports.

findings.title: Import groups are out of prescribed order
findings.standard_violated: sk-code code organization: imports order is Node built-ins, third-party packages, local modules, then type-only imports.
findings.fix_hint: Put node: imports before better-sqlite3, then local imports, then type-only imports.

findings.title: Internal any interop lacks justification comment
findings.standard_violated: sk-code TypeScript quality: permitted any usage must include a justification comment.
findings.fix_hint: Prefer a narrow parser interface; otherwise add a durable interop justification above each suppression.

findings.title: Large module mixes parser, resolver, finalizer, and indexing responsibilities
findings.standard_violated: sk-code universal patterns and code organization: keep modules small, behavior-focused, and single-purpose; files over recommended size should be split by concern.
findings.fix_hint: Split parser selection, module resolution, finalization, and scan orchestration into focused modules.

findings.title: Possible ephemeral recovery-procedure IDs in code comment
findings.standard_violated: Uncertain applicability: comment hygiene forbids perishable task, requirement, checklist, packet, phase, ADR, ticket, or finding IDs in code comments.
findings.fix_hint: If CG-RP-* is spec-local, replace it with durable behavior names; if stable API terminology, document that outside the comment.

findings.title: Ephemeral finding/spec ids in comments
findings.standard_violated: Comment hygiene HARD BLOCK: no spec/packet/phase/task/checklist/requirement/finding ids in code comments; keep durable WHY only.
findings.fix_hint: Remove F017/F-P*/Spec/T* labels from comments and keep only the durable rationale.

findings.title: Ephemeral review/spec ids in comments
findings.standard_violated: Comment hygiene HARD BLOCK: no spec/packet/phase/task/checklist/requirement/finding ids in code comments; keep durable WHY only.
findings.fix_hint: Replace review/spec/task ids with plain-language behavior constraints.

findings.title: Ephemeral review ids in Python comments
findings.standard_violated: Comment hygiene HARD BLOCK: no spec/packet/phase/task/checklist/requirement/finding ids in code comments; keep durable WHY only.
findings.fix_hint: Drop R5-P2 identifiers and keep the stable validation reason.

findings.title: Missing TypeScript module header
findings.standard_violated: TypeScript checklist P0: every TypeScript file has a module header identifying the module.
findings.fix_hint: Add the OpenCode TypeScript MODULE header before imports.

findings.title: Missing Python component header
findings.standard_violated: Python checklist P0: Python files start with shebang plus COMPONENT comment block before the module docstring.
findings.fix_hint: Add the required COMPONENT header between the shebang and docstring.

findings.title: Scoped write guard is not canonical
findings.standard_violated: Universal checklist P0 Filesystem Mutation Safety: file/folder mutations use realpath/canonical path checks and boundary-safe containment.
findings.fix_hint: Canonicalize the allowed root and nearest existing target parent before mkdir/write, then enforce path.relative containment.

findings.title: Python functions lack type hints
findings.standard_violated: Python checklist P1: all function signatures have type hints.
findings.fix_hint: Add parameter and return annotations to public/internal function signatures.

findings.title: Local require outside import section
findings.standard_violated: sk-code OpenCode code organization requires dependencies in the import section, ordered built-in, third-party, local.
findings.fix_hint: Move the fanout-salvage require into section 1 with the other local requires.

findings.title: Type-only import is not last
findings.standard_violated: sk-code TypeScript import order says type-only imports are separate and always last.
findings.fix_hint: Move the import type line after all runtime imports.

findings.title: Third-party import precedes Node built-ins
findings.standard_violated: sk-code universal import order requires built-in modules before third-party packages.
findings.fix_hint: Place node:fs/node:path/node:url imports before better-sqlite3.

findings.title: Unnumbered TypeScript section divider
findings.standard_violated: sk-code TypeScript and universal checklists require numbered ALL-CAPS section dividers.
findings.fix_hint: Use numbered dividers such as 1. TYPE DEFINITIONS, 2. CONSTANTS.

findings.title: Exported class lacks TSDoc
findings.standard_violated: sk-code TypeScript checklist requires TSDoc on exported classes.
findings.fix_hint: Add a short TSDoc summary before the exported error class.

findings.title: Exported interface lacks TSDoc
findings.standard_violated: sk-code TypeScript checklist requires TSDoc on exported interfaces.
findings.fix_hint: Add TSDoc summaries for exported public data contracts.

findings.title: TypeScript header is not the prescribed block
findings.standard_violated: sk-code TypeScript style guide prescribes a 3-line MODULE header block.
findings.fix_hint: Use the standard 3-line TypeScript MODULE banner.

findings.title: Comment starts lowercase
findings.standard_violated: sk-code JavaScript commenting rules require comment text to start with a capital letter.
findings.fix_hint: Capitalize the comment or remove it if it adds no durable why.

findings.title: Missing shell strict mode
findings.standard_violated: sk-code Shell Checklist P0 Strict Mode and alignment verifier SH-STRICT-MODE require `set -euo pipefail` near the top of shell scripts.
findings.evidence: .opencode/bin/worktree-guard.sh:1 flagged by `verify_alignment_drift.py --root .opencode/bin` as `[SH-STRICT-MODE] [ERROR] Missing `set -euo pipefail` strict mode declaration.
findings.fix_hint: Add `set -euo pipefail` immediately after the shebang/header.

findings.title: Missing JavaScript boxed header
findings.standard_violated: sk-code JavaScript Checklist P0 Box Header and JavaScript Style Guide §2 require a boxed component header before `'use strict'` for `.js/.cjs` files.
findings.evidence: .opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:1 starts directly with `'use strict';` and has no component header.
findings.fix_hint: Add the required boxed component/purpose header before `'use strict';`.

findings.title: Non-boxed JavaScript header
findings.standard_violated: sk-code JavaScript Checklist P0 Box Header and JavaScript Style Guide §2 require a boxed component header for `.js/.cjs` files.
findings.evidence: .opencode/bin/lib/launcher-session-proxy.cjs:1 uses a one-line bracketed comment instead of the required boxed header.
findings.fix_hint: Replace the top comment with the boxed component/purpose header and keep `'use strict';` immediately after it.

findings.title: Non-boxed launcher header
findings.standard_violated: sk-code JavaScript Checklist P0 Box Header and JavaScript Style Guide §2 require a boxed component header for `.js/.cjs` files.
findings.evidence: .opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:2 begins a plain bracketed launcher comment, not the required boxed component header.
findings.fix_hint: Convert the launcher preamble to the standard boxed header after the shebang.

findings.title: Non-boxed launcher header
findings.standard_violated: sk-code JavaScript Checklist P0 Box Header and JavaScript Style Guide §2 require a boxed component header for `.js/.cjs` files.
findings.evidence: .opencode/bin/mk-skill-advisor-launcher.cjs:2 begins a plain bracketed launcher comment, not the required boxed component header.
findings.fix_hint: Convert the launcher preamble to the standard boxed header after the shebang.

findings.title: Non-boxed launcher header
findings.standard_violated: sk-code JavaScript Checklist P0 Box Header and JavaScript Style Guide §2 require a boxed component header for `.js/.cjs` files.
findings.evidence: .opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:2 begins a plain bracketed launcher comment, not the required boxed component header.
findings.fix_hint: Convert the launcher preamble to the standard boxed header after the shebang.

findings.title: Dashed header instead of boxed header
findings.standard_violated: sk-code JavaScript Checklist P0 Box Header and JavaScript Style Guide §2 require a boxed component header for `.js/.cjs` files.
findings.evidence: .opencode/bin/code-index.cjs:2 uses a dashed header block rather than the required boxed JavaScript header.
findings.fix_hint: Replace the dashed component/purpose block with the boxed header format.

findings.title: Dashed header instead of boxed header
findings.standard_violated: sk-code JavaScript Checklist P0 Box Header and JavaScript Style Guide §2 require a boxed component header for `.js/.cjs` files.
findings.evidence: .opencode/bin/spec-memory.cjs:2 uses a dashed header block rather than the required boxed JavaScript header.
findings.fix_hint: Replace the dashed component/purpose block with the boxed header format.

findings.title: Missing required JS file structure
findings.standard_violated: sk-code JavaScript checklist P0: file has a box header with component identification and numbered ALL-CAPS sections; JavaScript style guide requires boxed header and section organization.
findings.evidence: .opencode/plugins/mk-spec-memory.js:1 starts with 'use strict'; no boxed component/purpose header or numbered section dividers are present.
findings.fix_hint: Add boxed component/purpose header and numbered IMPORTS/CONSTANTS/PLUGIN FACTORY sections.

findings.title: Missing required JS file structure
findings.standard_violated: sk-code JavaScript checklist P0: file has a box header with component identification and numbered ALL-CAPS sections; JavaScript style guide requires boxed header and section organization.
findings.evidence: .opencode/plugins/session-cleanup.js:1 starts with a shebang and prose block; no boxed component/purpose header or numbered section dividers are present.
findings.fix_hint: Use the OpenCode plugin box header and numbered sections; keep a shebang only if this is actually a CLI entrypoint.

findings.title: CommonJS used in ESM plugin
findings.standard_violated: sk-code JavaScript quality standards §2 and JavaScript checklist P1: .opencode/plugins/* must follow the OpenCode plugin ESM loader path with export default.
findings.evidence: .opencode/plugins/session-cleanup.js:17 uses require(), .opencode/plugins/session-cleanup.js:20 uses __dirname, while .opencode/plugins/session-cleanup.js:23 exports default.
findings.fix_hint: Use ESM imports and derive the directory from import.meta.url/fileURLToPath.

findings.title: Export lacks JSDoc
findings.standard_violated: sk-code JavaScript quality standards §5 / JavaScript checklist P2: public or exported functions have JSDoc documentation.
findings.evidence: .opencode/plugins/mk-code-graph.js:168 exports parseTransportPlan without a preceding JSDoc block.
findings.fix_hint: Add a short JSDoc block documenting input, return value, and invalid-payload behavior.

findings.title: Default export lacks JSDoc
findings.standard_violated: sk-code JavaScript quality standards §5 / JavaScript checklist P2: public or exported functions have JSDoc documentation.
findings.evidence: .opencode/plugins/mk-spec-memory.js:210 exports the plugin factory without a preceding JSDoc block.
findings.fix_hint: Add JSDoc for the plugin context, options, and returned hooks/tools object.

findings.title: Lowercase comment sentence
findings.standard_violated: sk-code JavaScript style guide §7: all comment text must start with a capital letter, except directives or technical identifiers.
findings.fix_hint: Capitalize the comment and keep the durable WHY.

findings.title: Lowercase comment sentence
findings.standard_violated: sk-code JavaScript style guide §7: all comment text must start with a capital letter, except directives or technical identifiers.
findings.evidence: .opencode/plugins/mk-skill-advisor.js:421 has '// in-flight promise dedup — concurrent identical-key requests share one bridge spawn'.
findings.fix_hint: Capitalize the comment or rewrite as a concise WHY comment.

findings.title: Missing shell strict mode
findings.standard_violated: Shell Quality Standards P0 Strict Mode: every shell script must enable `set -euo pipefail`; alignment verifier enforces SH-STRICT-MODE.
findings.fix_hint: Add `set -euo pipefail` near the top before `exec`.

findings.title: Hook uses partial strict mode
findings.standard_violated: Shell Quality Standards P0 Strict Mode: every shell script must enable `set -euo pipefail`.
findings.fix_hint: Use full strict mode and keep advisory exits explicit.

findings.title: Hook uses partial strict mode
findings.standard_violated: Shell Quality Standards P0 Strict Mode: every shell script must enable `set -euo pipefail`.
findings.fix_hint: Use full strict mode and guard intentional non-blocking commands.

findings.title: Missing required component header
findings.standard_violated: Shell Checklist P0 File Header: shell files need the standard COMPONENT/SCRIPT header block.
findings.fix_hint: Replace the one-line header with the standard shell header block.

findings.title: Script-level state uses lowercase globals
findings.standard_violated: Shell Style Guide Naming: constants and script-level variables use UPPER_SNAKE_CASE.
findings.fix_hint: Rename script-level arrays/state to UPPER_SNAKE_CASE or localize them.

findings.title: Uses POSIX test brackets
findings.standard_violated: Shell Style Guide Conditional Expressions: ALWAYS use `[[ ]]` over `[ ]`.
findings.fix_hint: Convert Bash conditionals from `[ ]` to `[[ ]]`.

findings.title: ShellCheck warnings remain
findings.standard_violated: Shell Quality Standards P2 ShellCheck Compliance: scripts should pass ShellCheck without warnings.
findings.fix_hint: Address SC2004 at 230/231 and SC2034 at 368.

findings.title: Oversized unsectioned shell module
findings.standard_violated: OpenCode code organization: significant files should use numbered sections; file-length guideline recommends splitting main entrypoints over roughly 400 LOC.
findings.fix_hint: Add numbered sections and consider extracting reusable helpers.

findings.title: Ephemeral IDs in YAML comments
findings.standard_violated: comment-hygiene HARD BLOCK / sk-code universal patterns: comments must not embed ADR-/REQ-/CHK-/task/finding IDs; keep durable WHY only
findings.fix_hint: Replace ADR/REQ/CHK/FIX labels with plain-language durable rationale.

findings.title: Ephemeral IDs in YAML comments
findings.standard_violated: comment-hygiene HARD BLOCK / sk-code universal patterns: comments must not embed ADR-/REQ-/CHK-/task/finding IDs; keep durable WHY only
findings.fix_hint: Remove REQ/ADR/CHK labels and keep the durable behavior rationale.

findings.title: Python script missing standard header
findings.standard_violated: sk-code OpenCode Python Quality Standards P0: every Python script must have the standard shebang plus component header block
findings.evidence: .opencode/commands/doctor/scripts/route-validate.py:1-2 starts with shebang then docstring; no component header block
findings.fix_hint: Add the standard boxed component header between shebang and module docstring.

findings.title: CJS script lacks boxed module header
findings.standard_violated: sk-code OpenCode JavaScript Style Guide: JavaScript files must use the boxed module header and .js/.cjs strict mode immediately after the header
findings.evidence: .opencode/commands/doctor/scripts/parent-skill-check.cjs:1-26 starts with shebang/JSDoc and puts 'use strict' at line 26; no boxed module header
findings.fix_hint: Use the standard JS boxed header after the shebang, then place 'use strict' before implementation code.

findings.title: Possible internal artifact IDs in comments
findings.standard_violated: sk-code universal comment policy: only durable external standards may be cited in comments; internal task/ticket/test IDs are perishable
findings.fix_hint: If these are internal regression/task IDs, replace them with durable rationale; keep only externally stable standards.
